title: Hemiangium excelsum
intro: Hemiangium excelsum, also known as the Chinese angelica tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Icacinaceae. Traditionally, Hemiangium excelsum has been used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Hemiangium excelsum is often made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Hemiangium excelsum is a species of plant in the family Icacinaceae. It is native to parts of Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and the Philippines. The classification of Hemiangium excelsum is as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Asterids, Order - Icacinales, Family - Icacinaceae, Genus - Hemiangium, Species - H. excelsum.
